title: Stale or Shared OT Service Accounts

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 1.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.13: Stale or Shared OT Service Accounts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.13' running on WinEventLog:Security password changes 4723/4724; AD snapshot via scripted input, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `WinEventLog:Security` password changes 4723/4724; AD snapshot via scripted input
spl:
index=windows EventCode IN ("4723","4724") earliest=-180d
| eval account=Target_User_Name
| where match(account,"(?i)scada|plc|hmi|svc_ot|batch")
| stats latest(_time) as last_pw_change by account
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_pw_change)/86400,0)
| where days_since>90 OR isnull(last_pw_change)
| join type=left account [ search index=ad_snapshot sourcetype="ad:user" | fields account, memberOf | eval grp_count=mvcount(memberOf) ]
| table account, days_since, grp_count
description: Surfaces OT accounts with weak rotation, excessive group breadth, or shared naming patterns inconsistent with IACS account management practice.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ad_snapshot, windows and sourcetypes ad:user. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ingest periodic LDAP snapshots to CSV; (2) define password rotation SLAs by tier; (3) eliminate shared accounts; (4) track exceptions with expiry dates. Indexes required: `index=ad_snapshot`, `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ad:user`.
visualization: Table, Bar (aging buckets), Single value (stale count). Key fields: `account`, `days_since`, `grp_count`, `last_pw_change`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for surfaces ot accounts with weak rotation, excessive group breadth, or shared naming patterns inconsistent with iacs account management practice. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` password changes 4723/4724; AD snapshot via scripted input.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest periodic LDAP snapshots to CSV; (2) define password rotation SLAs by tier; (3) eliminate shared accounts; (4) track exceptions with expiry dates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows EventCode IN ("4723","4724") earliest=-180d
| eval account=Target_User_Name
| where match(account,"(?i)scada|plc|hmi|svc_ot|batch")
| stats latest(_time) as last_pw_change by account
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_pw_change)/86400,0)
| where days_since>90 OR isnull(last_pw_change)
| join type=left account [ search index=ad_snapshot sourcetype="ad:user" | fields account, memberOf | eval grp_count=mvcount(memberOf) ]
| table account, days_since, grp_count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Stale or Shared OT Service Accounts (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.3)** — Surfaces OT accounts with weak rotation, excessive group breadth, or shared naming patterns inconsistent with IACS account management practice.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` password changes 4723/4724; AD snapshot via scripted input. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **account** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(account,"(?i)scada|plc|hmi|svc_ot|batch")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **account**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_since>90 OR isnull(last_pw_change)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Stale or Shared OT Service Accounts (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.3)** — Surfaces OT accounts with weak rotation, excessive group breadth, or shared naming patterns inconsistent with IACS account management practice.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` password changes 4723/4724; AD snapshot via scripted input. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: account, days_since, grp_count, last_pw_change.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar (aging buckets), Single value (stale count).

Scripted input (generic example)
This use case relies on a scripted input. In the app's local/inputs.conf add a stanza such as:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/YourApp/bin/collect.sh]
interval = 300
sourcetype = your_sourcetype
index = main
disabled = 0
```

The script should print one event per line (e.g. key=value). Example minimal script (bash):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Output metrics or events, one per line
echo "metric=value timestamp=$(date +%s)"
```

For full details (paths, scheduling, permissions), see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md
